answer_key.q1: B. It is beneficial for both students and the environment.
answer_key.q2: D. It aims to inspire new ways of thinking about the environment.

passages.title: Biophilic Design
passages.source_html:
Biophilic Design


A


Biophilic design, a movement related to green architecture, has gained much momentum within the building community in recent years. The premise of biophilic design seeks both to avoid or minimize harmful impacts on the natural environment, and equally importantly, to provide and restore beneficial contacts between people and nature in the built environment.


B


People have a psychologically developed need to commune with nature but this has frequently been neglected. All too often, architects have put creative originality before the needs of the people who have to live and work in their futuristic constructions, with scant regard for emotional, as well as practical, considerations. Biophilic design does not advocate tree houses or cave-dwelling, but it does provide the nature-based features that maximize human functioning and health. Though not technically a biophilic design, Fallingwater – the stunning house in rural Pennsylvania designed in the 1930s by Frank Lloyd Wright – arguably speaks to the human soul much more than the box-like blocks of flats designed by his contemporary Le Corbusier.


C


In the modern world, much of our built environment is obstructing the age-old connection between humans and nature. The rates of technological progress are far exceeding rates of psychological evolution, leaving us ill-equipped to cope with our lifestyle, which in turn leads to increased stress. Because biophilia attempts to integrate basic and current needs, it can alleviate the difficulties caused by the brain's constant attempts to function in a modern environment it has not yet evolved to handle. For example, one crucial element of the natural landscape to human health is sunlight. We are evolutionarily programmed to respond positively to sunny areas over dark or overcast ones, and sense that they will foster restoration, improve emotional well-being and promote health. 


D


Because of its tremendous impact biophilic design plays a vital role in healthcare and healthcare delivery. The current healthcare system contains many flaws, especially in its physical clinics, and offices are high-stress environments for patients, visitors and healthcare professionals alike. One well-known study looked at the impact of nature on patients after surgery. One group had a view of tree; the others had a window looking onto a brick wall. The first group had shorter hospital stays, received fewer negative comments from the nurses, required fewer analgesics, and had slightly fewer post-operative complications


E


As a consequence of this and other studies, nature and nature-based design have been integrated into the physical design of many hospitals. For example, Dartmouth Hitchcock Medical Center (DHMC) in New Hampshire boasts an atrium design, illuminating the entire facility. Natural elements also permeate the building, including wood, stone, and numerous live plants. While DHMC was built to incorporate these qualities, other hospitals have had biophilic features added to existing structures. The application of biophilia’s concepts to interior design in hospitals has increased substantially as administrators have witnessed patients' positive responses to nature.


F


Many existing buildings contain biophilic elements, but only a few have been built with the specific idea of biophilic design in mind. One such building is the Adam Joseph Lewis Center for Environmental Studies at Oberlin College, in Ohio. The director of Oberlin’s Environmental Studies at Oberlin Studies Program, David Orr, explained the building’s goals were ‘to create not just a place for classes but rather a building that would help to redefine the relationship between humankind and the environment – one that would expand our sense of ecological possibilities’. The Lewis Center is sustainable in a broader sense than in the typical application of the word. It harnesses solar power, utilizes both active and passive air systems, and monitors the weather to adapt to conditions. The Center's ‘Living Machine’ treats wastewater by combining traditional wastewater technology with wetland ecosystems’ purification processes, producing water that can be used in the toilets and for irrigation. In their design, Orr and his team of architects engineered a healthy and comfortable space for students, while ensuring the surrounding environment was undamaged.


G


Another example is the University of Guelph-Humber building in Ontario, Canada. It contains a centrally located bio-wall, vertically spanning the building. The wall is covered in dense foliage which not only connects people to nature on the inside of the building, but also functions as a new filtration system prototype. The wall purifies the air and has the potential to fulfill the building's fresh air intake requirements. This is another example of how biophilic design principles can be applied in a variety of contexts. These examples prove that the built environment need does not interfere with biological human needs to commune with nature, nor with existing ecological systems.


H


Ancient architects built for their cultures, which were almost always more in touch with the earth than western society of the present. They mimicked nature’s forms, producing magnificent structures with which we are still awed – though biophilic design is a novel concept, they certainly employed some of its recommendations. Today, we can add another layer to this tradition and ensure maximal benefit for our planet and ourselves.

groups.title: Questions 14-26
groups.source_html:
Questions 14 - 19


Look at the eight paragraphs (A - H). Which paragraph contains the following information?

You may choose any letter more than once.

Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
14

14 a description of how rapid change has a negative effect on people
✅ 正确答案：C

题目意思：哪个段落描述描述了快速变化对人类产生负面影响。 

 

定位关键信息：“rapid change”和“negative effect” 

 

对应原文：C段，“The rates of technological progress are far exceeding rates of psychological evolution, leaving us ill-equipped to cope with our lifestyle, which in turn leads to increased stress.” 

 

解题思路：原文提到科技进步的速度超过了心理进化的速度，导致我们无法适应当前的生活方式，增加了压力，符合“快速变化对人类的负面影响”。  
15

15 a reference to an architect whose designs were uncharacteristic of biophilia
✅ 正确答案：B

题目意思：哪个段落提到了一位设计风格不符合生物亲和设计理念的建筑师。  


定位关键信息：“architect”和“uncharacteristic of biophilia”


对应原文：B段提到，“though not technically a biophilic design, Fallingwater – the stunning house in rural Pennsylvania designed in the 1930s by Frank Lloyd Wright – arguably speaks to the human soul much more than the box-like blocks of flats designed by his contemporary Le Corbusier.”  


解题思路：Le Corbusier设计的建筑风格不符合生物亲和设计，而Frank Lloyd Wright的作品却更符合该理念。
16

16 a definition of the two main aims of biophilia
✅ 正确答案：A

题目意思：哪个段落描述了生物亲和设计的两个主要目标。  


定位关键信息：“main aims”和“biophilia”


对应原文：A段，“The premise of biophilic design seeks both to avoid or minimize harmful impacts on the natural environment, and equally importantly, to provide and restore beneficial contacts between people and nature in the built environment.”  


解题思路：原文中明确提到生物亲和设计的两个主要目标：减少对自然环境的有害影响，以及恢复人与自然的联系。
17

17 a positive claim about early forms of architecture
✅ 正确答案：H

题目意思：哪个段落描述了对早期建筑形式的积极评价。  


定位关键信息：“positive claim”和“early forms of architecture”  


对应原文：H段，“They mimicked nature’s forms, producing magnificent structures with which we are still awed.”

  

解题思路：原文对早期建筑形式进行积极评价，提到这些建筑模仿自然形态，并且至今令人惊叹。
18

18 a reference to the fact that many architects are too focused on innovation
✅ 正确答案：B

题目意思：哪个段落提到了许多建筑师过于注重创新。  


定位关键信息：“too focused on innovation”


对应原文：B段，“All too often, architects have put creative originality before the needs of the people who have to live and work in their futuristic constructions.”


解题思路：原文提到建筑师将“创造性”放在了人们需求之前，过度关注设计创新。  
19

19 a description of features which conserve energy in a biophilic design
✅ 正确答案：F

审题：题目问的是哪个段落讲述了在生物亲和性设计中的节能特性。


定位关键信息：“features which conserve energy”


对应原文：F段，“The Lewis Center is sustainable in a broader sense than in the typical application of the word. It harnesses solar power, utilizes both active and passive air systems, and monitors the weather to adapt to conditions.”


解题思路：F段提到Lewis Center利用太阳能，使用主动和被动空气系统，并监测天气以适应条件，这些都是节能特性。

Questions 20 - 24


Complete the sentences. Write ONE WORD ONLY in each gap.


20

In the modern world, advancements are happening so quickly that humans are unable to adjust to them.

正确答案： 20 technological
Question 20

题目意思：在现代世界中，什么进步速度太快，人类难以适应。

 

定位关键信息：“modern world”和“advancements”


对应原文：C段，“The rates of technological progress are far exceeding rates of psychological evolution.”  


解题思路：原文提到“technological progress”超过了“心理进化”的速度，说明人类难以适应科技进步的速度。  

21

Biophilia can help us deal with the caused by the difficulties of our current lifestyles.

正确答案： 21 stress
Question 21

题目意思：生物亲和设计可以帮助我们应对由当前生活方式带来的什么。  


定位关键信息：“deal with”和“current lifestyles”


对应原文：C段，“The rates of technological progress are far exceeding rates of psychological evolution, leaving us ill-equipped to cope with our lifestyle, which in turn leads to increased stress.”  


解题思路：原文提到“biophilia”可以缓解由当前生活方式引起的“stress”（压力）。

22

People instinctively feel more comfortable in places which have plenty of .

正确答案： 22 sunlight
Question 22

题目意思：人们本能地在有充足什么的地方感到更舒适。  


定位关键信息：“comfortable”和“plenty of”  


对应原文：C段，“We are evolutionarily programmed to respond positively to sunny areas over dark or overcast ones.”  


解题思路：原文提到人类对“sunny areas”有积极反应，表明阳光充足的地方使人更舒适。

23

An experiment designed to test the effect of nature on patient recovery times found that those who could see a made a faster recovery.  

正确答案： 23 tree
Question 23

题目意思：一个测试自然对病人恢复时间影响的实验发现，能够看到什么的病人恢复更快。  

定位关键信息：“experiment”和“effect of nature”和“recovery”  


对应原文：D段，“One group had a view of tree; the others had a window looking onto a brick wall. The first group had shorter hospital stays, received fewer negative comments from the nurses, required fewer analgesics, and had slightly fewer post-operative complications.”  


解题思路：原文提到能够看到树的病人恢复更快。  

24

There is a biophilic design feature in the University of Guelph-Humber building which improves the quality of the  .

正确答案： 24 air
Question 24

题目意思：在Guelph-Humber大学建筑中有一个生物亲和设计的特点，可以改善什么的质量。  


定位关键信息：“ the University of Guelph-Humber”和“biophilic design feature”和“quality”  


对应原文：G段，“The wall purifies the air and has the potential to fulfill the building's fresh air intake requirements.”  


解题思路：原文提到“the wall purifies the air”，说明该设计特征可以改善空气质量。  

Questions 25-26


Choose TWO correct answers.


Options (Word Bank):
A. It was the first educational establishment designed with biophilic principles.
B. It is beneficial for both students and the environment.
C. It has established new concepts for classroom design.
D. It aims to inspire new ways of thinking about the environment.
E. It provides solar power to other buildings in the surrounding area.
25

Which TWO of the following statements does the writer make about the Adam Joseph Lewis Center at Oberlin College?

正确答案： 1 B. It is beneficial for both students and the environment.  |  2 D. It aims to inspire new ways of thinking about the environment.

题目意思：以下哪个选项是关于Oberlin学院的Adam Joseph Lewis中心的描述？

  

定位关键信息：“Adam Joseph Lewis Center at Oberlin College”


对应原文：F段，“The director of Oberlin’s Environmental Studies at Oberlin Studies Program, David Orr, explained the building’s goals were ‘to create not just a place for classes but rather a building that would help to redefine the relationship between humankind and the environment – one that would expand our sense of ecological possibilities’. The Lewis Center is sustainable in a broader sense than in the typical application of the word. It harnesses solar power, utilizes both active and passive air systems, and monitors the weather to adapt to conditions.”  


解题思路：原文中提到建筑不仅有利于学生，还保护了环境；同时，该建筑旨在重新定义人与环境的关系，启发新的环保思维。
